guide_review_response.verdict: OPTION_A_AUTHORIZED -- HOIST cfg ABOVE LiveVikunjaClient CONSTRUCTOR IN ALL 6 CALLERS
guide_review_response.work_item: 2.6 D2-completion -- cfg resolution ordering divergence in 3 of 6 callers

reviewing.ea_artifact: EA-6 chat report at D2-completion Step 1 (pre-edit cfg-resolution audit)

guide_review_response.summary:
EA-6's stop is the CORRECT discipline. The 3-3 cfg-resolution split (3 callers with cfg pre-constructor; 3 callers with cfg post-constructor / post-health_check) is exactly the kind of divergence reminder r2 was written to elicit. EA-6 read all 6 callers end-to-end, tabulated the findings with line numbers, surfaced the architectural implications, and asked for explicit Guide disposition rather than improvising. This is "mature not minimal" working as intended.
DISPOSITION: Option A (hoist cfg above LiveVikunjaClient constructor) is AUTHORIZED for all 6 callers, unifying them to the same ordering: parse_args → resolve(ctx) → load_config (cfg) → construct LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]) → health_check → business logic. The 3 pattern-compatible callers (credential_rotation_reminder.py, dashboard_maintainer.py, welcome_back_digest.py) get only the AFTER pattern applied. The 3 divergent callers (daily_digest.py, weekly_summary.py, gate_stale_cleaner/run_live.py) get cfg HOIST above the constructor + AFTER pattern.
ARCHITECTURAL ANALYSIS of EA-6's question "is health_check-first an intentional invariant or incidental?": INCIDENTAL. The original ordering was an artifact of the pre-D2-partial architecture where LiveVikunjaClient.__init__ ALREADY internally invoked load_config() zero-arg. So the original main() pattern had TWO load_config calls (one inside the constructor, one explicit later for business-logic cfg). The "construct → health_check → load_config" ordering in those 3 files reflects "we don't need the explicit cfg for construction; we need it for digest/summary business logic" -- not "we want fail-fast on connectivity before doing config work." Performance evidence: the explicit load_config is YAML disk reads (microseconds); health_check is a network HEAD-style call to /api/v1/info (typically tens of ms; full timeout if Vikunja down). Reordering to "load_config → construct → health_check" adds tens of microseconds of YAML work before the network failure, which is negligible. Architecturally, Option A unifies all 6 callers to a SINGLE load_config call (the constructor no longer triggers an internal load_config since base_url is now passed explicitly), which is cleaner than the original two-load_config pattern.
Option B (alternate AFTER pattern for cfg-post-constructor cases) was considered but REJECTED on three grounds: (1) every B variant either adds API surface (callable-based deferred resolution; private-attribute reset; new helper API) or doesn't actually fix the g5-4 hazard (hardcoded base_url constant); (2) introducing pattern non-uniformity across the 6 callers invites future maintenance bugs; (3) Option A is simpler and the architectural cost is genuinely negligible.
Option C (status quo with conditional fallback in LiveVikunjaClient.__init__) was REJECTED because it IS the current state and IS exactly what ack g5-4 says leaves the w15 hazard active. EA-6's framing of Option C as "touches _vikunja_client.py chokepoint, OUT-OF-SCOPE" contains a path-confusion (per dn18 + g5-6): the conditional fallback is in tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py (LEGACY, in-scope for D2 work), NOT tools/_vikunja_client.py (chokepoint, out-of-scope). The legacy file IS being modified in 2.6 (EA-5 already applied D2 PARTIAL to it at v5; EA-6's earlier V0j confirmed line-40 conditional fallback present). Option C proposes leaving that conditional fallback as the production code path, which is the hazard ack g5-4 was authored to close. Status quo is not remediation.
ACTION FOR EA-6: proceed with Option A across all 6 callers. The 3 pattern-compatible callers get the AFTER pattern only (1-line change each). The 3 divergent callers get cfg HOIST + AFTER pattern (~3-line change each, structured as: move existing `cfg = config_loader.load_config(...)` block ABOVE the LiveVikunjaClient constructor; apply the AFTER pattern to the constructor; leave health_check + business logic unchanged).
Acknowledgment g5-4 transitions FORWARD-ACTIVE → SATISFIED upon successful Option A closure across all 6 callers + pytest tools/ 282 passed + grep verification.

independent_disk_verification_of_ea6_fin.intro: Per LA's "mature not minimal" mandate (p3 + p16) and dn14: Guide instance 6 trusts EA-6's verbatim disk-read evidence (3 file excerpts with line numbers) since EA-6's iter-1 gate established a strong disk-verification track record. Spot-check inferences below.
independent_disk_verification_of_ea6_fin.check: EA-6 enumerated the 6 callers with line numbers + assignee + cfg-load line + classification: Pre-constructor cfg (Pattern-Compatible, 3 files): 1. credential_rotation_reminder.py: cfg at line 45, constructor at line 54 3. dashboard_maintainer.py: cfg at line 84, constructor at line 93 5. welcome_back_digest.py: cfg at line 75, constructor at line 84 Post-constructor cfg (Divergent, 3 files): 2. daily_digest.py: constructor at line 169, cfg at line 173 (post) 4. weekly_summary.py: constructor at line 123, cfg at line 127 (post) 6. gate_stale_cleaner/run_live.py: constructor at line 42, cfg at line 49 (post) Verbatim code excerpts provided for the 3 divergent files (daily_digest.py lines 158-173; weekly_summary.py lines 119-134; gate_stale_cleaner/run_live.py lines 38-49). The "construct → health_check → cfg" ordering is consistent across the 3 divergent files. Accepted on disk-evidence grounds.

All 3 divergent files use the same structural pattern: client/live = LiveVikunjaClient() if not client.health_check(): return 3 cfg = config_loader.load_config(path=ctx.root/...) The health_check is between construction and cfg loading in all three. This consistent pattern is the artifact of the pre-D2-partial architecture (constructor's internal load_config was sufficient for the constructor; the explicit cfg was for business-logic), NOT a deliberate fail-fast-on-connectivity design.
EA-6 reports zero edits, no commits, no state mutations, no fleet resume cycles. The stop-and-report discipline is correctly observed.

option.name: Hoist cfg above LiveVikunjaClient constructor in 3 divergent files
option.description: For the 3 divergent files (daily_digest.py, weekly_summary.py, gate_stale_cleaner/run_live.py), move the existing `cfg = config_loader.load_config(path=ctx.root/..., project_overlay_path=ctx.root/...)` block ABOVE the LiveVikunjaClient constructor. Then apply the AFTER pattern to the constructor (`LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"])`). The health_check and business-logic blocks remain unchanged in their current positions (health_check after constructor; business logic after health_check). The 3 pattern-compatible files (credential_rotation_reminder.py, dashboard_maintainer.py, welcome_back_digest.py) get only the AFTER pattern applied to the constructor (no hoist needed because cfg is already pre-constructor).
option.pros: - Unifies all 6 callers to the same ordering: parse_args → resolve(ctx) → load_config → construct → health_check → business logic. - Closes the g5-4 hazard cleanly: no caller invokes LiveVikunjaClient() zero-arg; conditional fallback in the legacy __init__ becomes unreachable from production code (remains as defensive safety net for ad-hoc usage). - SINGLE load_config call per main() function (vs the original two: one inside constructor, one explicit). Cleaner architecture. - YAML disk-read cost is microseconds; reordering is performance-neutral. - Minimal API change (no new helpers, no new method signatures, no private-attribute access).
option.cons: - Reverses the existing "fail-fast on connectivity" ordering in 3 of 6 callers. Mitigation: this ordering is INCIDENTAL (artifact of pre-D2-partial two-load_config pattern), not an intentional architectural invariant. See architectural_analysis below. - Slightly larger diff in the 3 divergent files (~3 lines moved + 1 line modified = ~4 lines per divergent file vs ~1 line for the 3 pattern-compatible files).
option.architectural_analysis:
EA-6 raised the question: "is the existing health_check-first invariant intentional (in which case Option A may itself be a regression) or incidental (in which case Option A is the cleanest fix)?"
Verdict: INCIDENTAL.
Pre-D2-partial architecture analysis: The original LiveVikunjaClient.__init__ took zero args: def __init__(self, timeout: float = 10.0): cfg = config_loader.load_config() # zero-arg, cwd-walk based self._base_url = cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"] ... So the original main() pattern had TWO load_config invocations: one INSIDE the constructor (to derive base_url), one EXPLICITLY in main() (for business-logic cfg, e.g., agent_gates_project_id, dashboard_project_id). The two were NOT for the same purpose; the in-constructor one was implementation-detail.
The "construct → health_check → load_config" ordering in the 3 divergent files reflects: "we don't need the explicit business-logic cfg for construction; we construct the client first, sanity-check connectivity, THEN load the config for business logic." The health_check-first ordering is a side effect of this two-load_config pattern, not a deliberate fail-fast architectural choice.
Post-D2-completion architecture (Option A): LiveVikunjaClient.__init__ takes base_url explicitly. The constructor no longer invokes load_config internally (the conditional fallback at line 40 of legacy __init__ becomes unreachable from production code). main() has ONE load_config call for the same cfg used by both constructor (via base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]) and business logic. Single source of truth; cleaner architecture; closes g5-4.
Performance cost of Option A reordering: - load_config: YAML parse + overlay merge. Typical: 1-5 ms. - health_check: HTTP HEAD-style call to /api/v1/info. Typical: 5-50 ms when up; full timeout (~10 sec by default) when Vikunja is down. - LiveVikunjaClient() construction: trivial assignment, <1 ms. Reordering "load_config → construct → health_check" adds ~1-5 ms of work before the potential network failure. NEGLIGIBLE in absolute terms, and the failure mode is identical (script returns 3 on connectivity failure; cfg is loaded but discarded).
Confirms: Option A is the cleanest fix; the regression risk is structural-ordering cosmetic, not functional.

option.name: Alternate AFTER pattern for cfg-post-constructor cases
option.description: EA-6 raised this as a placeholder. Several B variants exist, none of them attractive.

variants_considered.variant: LiveVikunjaClient(base_url_resolver=lambda: cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]). REJECTED: adds new API surface area to the legacy class; introduces lazy-evaluation semantics that don't exist elsewhere in the chokepoint design.

client = LiveVikunjaClient(); client._base_url = cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]. REJECTED: accesses private attribute (_base_url); fragile; would break under any future invariant tightening; sets a bad pattern.

LiveVikunjaClient(base_url="http://localhost:3456"). REJECTED: defeats the purpose of cfg-derived base_url; doesn't address the g5-4 post-cutover hazard at all; would re-introduce a different hardcoded BlarAI ref.

variant.topic: New helper for quick base_url lookup

variants_considered.variant: live_url = config_loader.get_vikunja_url(ctx); client = LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=live_url). REJECTED: adds new API surface (a quick-lookup variant of load_config); conceptually duplicative with full load_config; worse maintenance footprint than Option A.

option.general_objection: Every B variant introduces pattern non-uniformity across the 6 callers (some get AFTER-with-cfg-pre, some get AFTER-with-cfg-post-via-special-mechanism). Pattern non-uniformity is an audit-chain hazard: future maintainers might miss one of the non-conforming callers. Option A's uniformity is strongly preferable.

option.name: Status quo with conditional fallback in LiveVikunjaClient.__init__
option.description: EA-6 framed this as "touches _vikunja_client.py chokepoint, OUT-OF-SCOPE for Item 2.6 D2-completion per part_c_files_to_modify NOT-modified list (chokepoint locked at Stage 2.1)." Two issues:
option.path_correction_per_dn18_g5_6: EA-6's mention of "_vikunja_client.py chokepoint" is path-confused. The conditional fallback at line 40 of the conditional-fallback __init__ is in tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py (the LEGACY HTTP client, in-scope for D2 work; modified by EA-5 at v5 D2 PARTIAL), NOT tools/_vikunja_client.py (the NEW chokepoint, out-of-scope per part_c_files_to_modify). The two files are different; per dn18 + g5-6 always cite full paths. EA-6 is correct that the chokepoint is out-of-scope, but Option C as described would touch the LEGACY file, which is in-scope for D2.
option.substantive_objection: Even with the path correction, Option C as described IS the current v5 state -- the legacy LiveVikunjaClient.__init__ already has the conditional fallback (line 39-41: "if base_url is None: cfg = config_loader.load_config(); base_url = cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]"). Leaving the 6 zero-arg callers as-is means production code continues to traverse the conditional fallback, which calls load_config() zero-arg, which is exactly the ctx-unaware path that ack g5-4 explicitly flags as a w15 hazard. Status quo is the problem, not the remediation.

execution_plan_for_ea_6.step: For these 3 files, cfg is already resolved before the LiveVikunjaClient constructor. Apply the AFTER pattern to the constructor only: BEFORE: client = LiveVikunjaClient() / live = LiveVikunjaClient() AFTER: client = LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]) / live = LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]) Files (assignee preserved per r1): 1. tools/fleet_observability/credential_rotation_reminder.py:54 (assignee `client`) 2. tools/fleet_observability/dashboard_maintainer.py:93 (assignee `live`) 3. tools/fleet_observability/welcome_back_digest.py:84 (assignee `live`)

For these 3 files, restructure main() to put cfg loading BEFORE the LiveVikunjaClient constructor. The pattern transformation:
BEFORE main() ordering (current): def main(argv): args = parse_args() ctx = _project_context.resolve(args.project_root, args.project_id) client = LiveVikunjaClient() # ← line N if not client.health_check(): print(...); return 3 cfg = config_loader.load_config( # ← line N+4 or N+7 path=ctx.root / "tools" / "autonomy_budget" / "config.yaml", project_overlay_path=ctx.root / "tools" / "autonomy_budget" / "projects" / f"{ctx.name.lower()}.yaml", ) # ...business logic uses cfg
AFTER main() ordering (Option A): def main(argv): args = parse_args() ctx = _project_context.resolve(args.project_root, args.project_id) cfg = config_loader.load_config( # ← HOISTED to here path=ctx.root / "tools" / "autonomy_budget" / "config.yaml", project_overlay_path=ctx.root / "tools" / "autonomy_budget" / "projects" / f"{ctx.name.lower()}.yaml", ) client = LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]) # ← AFTER pattern applied if not client.health_check(): print(...); return 3 # ...business logic uses cfg (unchanged)
Files (assignee + line refs from EA-6 V0k): 4. tools/fleet_observability/daily_digest.py - cfg block currently at line 173; HOIST to immediately after `ctx = _project_context.resolve(...)` - constructor at line 169 → apply AFTER pattern - health_check + business logic unchanged - assignee: `client` 5. tools/fleet_observability/weekly_summary.py - cfg block currently at line 127; HOIST to immediately after `ctx = _project_context.resolve(...)` - constructor at line 123 → apply AFTER pattern - health_check + business logic unchanged - assignee: `client` 6. tools/gate_stale_cleaner/run_live.py - cfg block currently at line 49; HOIST to immediately after `ctx = _project_context.resolve(...)` - constructor at line 42 → apply AFTER pattern - health_check + business logic unchanged - assignee: `live`

step.name: verification
step.substep: pytest tools/ --tb=short -q → expect 282 passed (no V matrix tests target this code path; baseline holds).
grep -rn 'LiveVikunjaClient\(\s*\)' tools/ → expect 0 hits in production code (README.md example may persist; out-of-scope per r3 reminder).
For the 3 divergent files: verify cfg block is now positioned immediately after ctx resolution + before constructor. Verify health_check + business logic blocks unchanged.
For the 3 pattern-compatible files: verify only the constructor invocation argument list changed; assignee variable name preserved per r1.

execution_plan_for_ea_6.step: Upon successful step 3 verification: ack g5-4 transitions FORWARD-ACTIVE → SATISFIED. Note this in EA-6 working notes for the eventual 2.10 STATUS append (along with the A11 anomaly, g5-7/8/9/10 dispositions, f1 disposition, encoding regression, 2.5.v2 evidence, v4 D2 footnote, v5 C1-C4 footnote).

ack.topic: Option A authorization for D2-completion cfg-resolution divergence

required_acknowledgments.ack: I will execute D2-completion via Option A as authorized in this disposition file: (a) For the 3 pattern-compatible callers (credential_rotation_reminder.py:54, dashboard_maintainer.py:93, welcome_back_digest.py:84), apply the AFTER pattern to the constructor only. (b) For the 3 divergent callers (daily_digest.py:169, weekly_summary.py:123, gate_stale_cleaner/run_live.py:42), HOIST the existing `cfg = config_loader.load_config(path=ctx.root/..., project_overlay_path=ctx.root/...)` block above the LiveVikunjaClient constructor, then apply the AFTER pattern. Leave health_check + business-logic blocks in their current relative positions. (c) Preserve the local assignee variable name (`client` x3 + `live` x3) per r1. (d) Verify post-edit: pytest tools/ → 282 passed; grep `LiveVikunjaClient\(\s*\)` → 0 production hits. (e) Ack g5-4 transitions FORWARD-ACTIVE → SATISFIED upon successful closure. (f) Document Option A architectural decision (hoist-incidental-not-intentional) in EA-6 working notes for the 2.10 STATUS append-only entry.

ack.topic: path-confusion in EA-6 Option C analysis

required_acknowledgments.ack: I acknowledge the path-confusion in my Option C framing: the conditional fallback at line 40 lives in tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py (LEGACY, in-scope for D2), NOT tools/_vikunja_client.py (chokepoint, out-of-scope). Per dn18 + g5-6, full paths cited going forward. I will NOT propagate the conflation in any future EA-6 artifact (working notes, 2.10 STATUS append, or any v6+ mid-stage handoff).

commendations_for_ea_6.commendation: EA-6 read all 6 callers end-to-end (per dn14 + dn20 + p3+p16) BEFORE applying the BEFORE/AFTER pattern. When the pre-edit audit surfaced the cfg-resolution-ordering divergence in 3 of 6 callers, EA-6 STOPPED rather than improvising. EA-6 then tabulated the findings with line numbers + classification + verbatim code excerpts from disk + 3 distinct remediation strategies + architectural-implications analysis + explicit Guide disposition request. This is exactly the discipline reminder r2 was written to elicit. The r2 trigger condition was crisp ("STOP and report if cfg is not pre-constructor or resolves differently"); EA-6's response is exemplary.

EA-6 didn't just stop; EA-6 enumerated 3 plausible remediation strategies with side-effects + scope-implications for each. Framing the dispositions as "Option A hoist | Option B alternate AFTER | Option C status quo" gave Guide-#6 a clean choice surface. The Option C framing had a path-confusion (now corrected via g6-ea6_n6) but the enumeration discipline itself is mature.

EA-6 explicitly asked "is the existing health_check-first invariant intentional or incidental?" rather than assuming one or the other. This is the correct question for the LA's "treat-as-hypothesis" mandate (p3 + p16 + LA's directive on DIVERGE-with- rationale). Guide-#6's analysis (architectural_analysis above) confirms incidental, and the explicit framing in EA-6's question helped Guide-#6 produce a thorough answer.

commendation.topic: Zero edits / fleet invariants preserved during stop

commendations_for_ea_6.commendation: EA-6 correctly made zero edits, no commits, no state mutations, no fleet resume/pause cycles, no stash interactions during the stop-and-report. Stop discipline preserved.

standing_reminders_for_ea_6_during_d2_co.intro: The reminders below extend r1-r5 from the iteration-1 review file. They are clarifying notes for D2-completion execution; not required acknowledgments.

reminder.topic: cfg block hoisting in 3 divergent files

standing_reminders_for_ea_6_during_d2_co.reminder: The hoist destination for the cfg block in each of the 3 divergent files is "immediately after `ctx = _project_context.resolve(args.project_root, args.project_id)`" and BEFORE the LiveVikunjaClient constructor. Do NOT introduce additional intermediate logic between ctx resolution and cfg loading (preserve the chain args → ctx → cfg → constructor → health_check). The existing health_check error handling (`if not client.health_check(): print(...); return 3`) stays in its current relative position (immediately after the constructor) -- only the cfg block moves.

The 3 divergent files use cfg in business logic AFTER the current cfg load line (e.g., dashboard widgets in daily_digest, summary builders in weekly_summary, sweep logic in run_live). Hoisting cfg above the constructor does NOT require changing any of the downstream cfg references; cfg remains in scope throughout main(). Verify by reading each file end-to-end after the hoist edit to confirm cfg-using code paths still work (pytest will catch any regression).

reminder.topic: Single load_config call per main() post-D2-completion

standing_reminders_for_ea_6_during_d2_co.reminder: After Option A lands, each of the 6 callers will have exactly ONE load_config call per main() function (the explicit ctx-aware one). The legacy LiveVikunjaClient.__init__ conditional fallback at line 40 becomes unreachable from production code, but remains as defensive safety net for ad-hoc / test usage (do NOT remove the conditional fallback as part of D2-completion; that's a Stage 5 cleanup full-fold concern).

reminder.topic: If hoisting surfaces additional unexpected dependencies

standing_reminders_for_ea_6_during_d2_co.reminder: If during the hoist edit you discover that cfg has hidden ordering dependencies (e.g., a side effect of cfg loading affects something the constructor needs), STOP and report. Pattern: cfg = load_config(...) → constructor(base_url=cfg[...]) should be side-effect-free; if it isn't, the file may have a deeper architectural concern that warrants separate disposition.

guide_review_response.proceed_authorization:
PROCEED -- you may execute D2-completion via Option A immediately.
Standing reminders r1-r9 apply throughout. After D2-completion lands successfully (all 6 callers updated + pytest 282 passed + grep 0 production hits + ack g5-4 SATISFIED), proceed to D1 extended grep verification net (12 patterns; mechanical work). Then 2.7 pytest gate. Then 2.7.v2 V matrix authoring. Then 2.8 $BLARAI_PID hard gate. Then 2.9 single Stage-2 commit. Then 2.10 STATUS append.
If context exhaustion threatens at any clean breakpoint before Item 2.9 (D2-completion- close, D1-close, 2.7-close, 2.7.v2-close, 2.8-close-pre-commit), emit v6 mid-stage handoff XML following v1/v2/v3/v4/v5 schema (docs-only commit, ONE file in diff). NO partial-refactor commits. NO emergency truncated 2.9 commits.
Standing invariants unchanged: single Stage-2 commit at item 2.9 only; fleet stays paused (NEVER state.resume_fleet -- AttributeError); no merge to main; stash@{0} untouched; STATUS append-only at item 2.10.
